case_ref: kp_0114 as drafted: 'same-store sales' vs 'same-store sales growth', drafter gold SAME
description: During K-pairs.v1 adjudication, one drafted SAME pair placed a metric against its own growth rate. The naming/unit rules under-determine pair identity for this shape: comps-style metrics are conventionally REPORTED as growth percentages (arguing SAME), but a level reading exists ('comparable sales of $4.2B') and UNIT-01/OD-11 treat growth bases as their own series families (arguing DIFFERENT). A grader refusing SAME here is defensible, so the gold is contestable either way - unacceptable in a calibration key where every gold must be bulletproof (a wrong DIFFERENT gold is fatal to the 0/110 bar; a contestable SAME gold unfairly burns the 10% false-refusal budget).
proposed_amendment: Before K-pairs.v2 mined-pair adjudication (mined pairs WILL contain metric-vs-own-growth shapes from the real catalog), pin the pair-identity rule for a metric vs its own growth/change rate: recommended default = DIFFERENT unless the corpus convention makes the growth reading the ONLY natural reading of both sides (comps-style). Owner/Fable to ratify at v2 lock.
resolution: v1 resolved by AVOIDANCE, not by pinning: kp_0114 reworded to a decisively-SAME abbreviation pair (same-store sales vs comps). No v1 gold rests on the unpinned boundary. The boundary question itself stays open for v2 (proposed_amendment).
rule: Pair-identity for metric vs its own growth rate is unpinned
why_two_ways: (A) comps-convention reading: the growth suffix adds nothing, SAME. (B) series-family reading: level and growth are different objects with different units, DIFFERENT. Both defensible from current locked text.
